name: Torre de Ses Portes — gravel loop through the Ses Salines d'Eivissa y Formentera N.P.
name_original: Torre de Ses Portes — bucle gravel por el P.N. de Ses Salines d’Eivissa y Formentera
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ibiza offer diverse landscapes, from rugged coastlines with views of the Mediterranean to tranquil inland roads through pine forests and rolling hills. The island features varied terrain, including flat routes along salt flats and beaches, as well as challenging hilly trails with significant elevation changes. Cyclists can explore areas rich in natural beauty and historical significance, including protected natural parks. The infrastructure supports a range of cycling abilities, providing both paved roads and gravel paths. Best touring cycling routes around Ibiza The most popular touring cycling route is El Pilar de la Mola Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain and offers scenic views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret - circular gravel in the Ses Salines Natural Park, a difficult 22.3 miles (35.8 km) path. This trail winds through the Ses Salines Natural Park, showcasing coastal landscapes and natural features. Local touring cyclists also love the Torre de Ses Portes — gravel loop through the Ses Salines d'Eivissa y Formentera N.P., a 16.8 miles (27.0 km) trail leading through the Ses Salines Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Ibiza is defined by rugged coastlines, tranquil pine forests, and rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Ibiza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 8000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ibiza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Ibiza?
A: Ibiza offers a wide array of touring cycling routes, with over 550 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs through mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Ibiza's touring cycling routes feature diverse terrain. You'll find rugged coastlines with Mediterranean views, tranquil inland roads winding through pine forests and rolling hills, and flat routes along salt flats and beaches. For a challenge, areas like Sa Serra Grossa offer significant elevation changes. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008543414/el-pilar-de-la-mola-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilar de la Mola Loop&lt;/a&gt; showcase varied landscapes, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009050741/sant-miquel-de-balansat-circular-gravel-through-the-interior-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel through the interior of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the island's picturesque countryside.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Yes, Ibiza has several easy and family-friendly touring cycling routes. The island offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013131667/beaches-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaches Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.3 km long and features minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for a relaxed ride. These routes often pass by beaches and quaint towns, providing plenty of sightseeing opportunities for all ages.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Ibiza offers routes with significant climbs and descents. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008531045/es-cap-de-barbaria-and-torre-des-garroveret-circular-gravel-in-the-ses-salines-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Es Cap de Barbaria and Torre des Garroveret - circular gravel in the Ses Salines Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 35.8 km path with over 280 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009026705/cala-tarida-valdella-y-d-hort-coves-of-the-west-coast-of-the-island-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Tarida, Valdella y d'Hort — coves of the west coast of the island of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 600 meters of ascent.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ibiza?
A: The best times for touring cycling in Ibiza are typically spring (February-May) and autumn (October-November). During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and there are fewer crowds, making for a more enjoyable cycling experience. The island's weather is generally favorable for outdoor activities during these periods.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Ibiza?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Ibiza are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the easy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009036007/torre-de-ses-portes-gravel-loop-through-the-ses-salines-d-eivissa-y-formentera-n-p" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Ses Portes — gravel loop through the Ses Salines d'Eivissa y Formentera N.P.&lt;/a&gt;, which is 27 km long, and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40316330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – La Mola Lighthouse loop from Platja de Migjorn&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Ibiza's touring cycling routes offer access to stunning natural features and historical landmarks. You can explore areas like the Ses Salines Natural Park, which features salt flats and beaches where flamingos are sometimes spotted. Routes also pass by impressive cliffs and secluded coves such as Cala Xarraca. Notable landmarks include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/601057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2995920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbària Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique geological formations like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/730372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Ibiza?
A: The komoot community highly rates touring cycling in Ibiza, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. Cyclists often praise the island's diverse landscapes, which include rugged coastlines, tranquil pine forests, and rolling hills. The variety of routes catering to different ability levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging climbs, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of Formentera?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes provide spectacular views of the neighboring island of Formentera. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Formentera – Salt Lagoon loop from Molí d’en Botigues&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic vistas across the water, often passing by coastal areas and natural parks.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the interior of Ibiza?
A: Absolutely. While many routes hug the coast, Ibiza also offers touring cycling paths that delve into its tranquil interior. These routes traverse picturesque countryside with rolling hills, charming villages, traditional fields, and pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009050741/sant-miquel-de-balansat-circular-gravel-through-the-interior-of-ibiza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel de Balansat — circular gravel through the interior of Ibiza&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you away from the busier coastal areas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by beaches?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Ibiza offer opportunities to visit beautiful beaches and coves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013131667/beaches-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beaches Route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that explores scenic valleys and beaches. You can also find routes that pass by popular spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/448087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ses Salines Beach&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to enjoy the coastal scenery.
tips:
Spanish island located in the Mediterranean Sea and which forms, together with those of Majorca, Menorca and Formentera and several smaller islets, the archipelago and autonomous community of the Balearic Islands.

The island is internationally renowned for the beauty of its coves and beaches, the quality of its waters, as well as its festivals and discos, which attract many tourists. The port area of Ibiza also attracts many tourists for its nightlife.

From the 8th century B.C. the coasts of Ibiza were commanded by Phoenician warriors, who, upon observing the vast territory of dunes, began to build small salt flats.

Since then, the production of salt has evolved over the years and each population, different in culture and religion, has preserved the production of salt on the beaches of Ibiza.

More information: https://ibizabotanicobiotecnologico.com/las-salinas-ibiza/
